channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain staves off Villa comeback to reach Champions League semifinals
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, England (AP) — Paris Saint-Germain staved off a gallant fightback by Aston Villa to reach the Champions League semifinals despite a 3-2 loss to the English club on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: April 15 is Jackie Robinson Day around Major League Baseball
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jackie Robinson’s legacy is being celebrated around the major leagues on Tuesday, with the day named for the first Black baseball player of the modern era and marking the 78th anniversary of his debut for the Brooklyn Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodger Stadium to host baseball, with surfing and cricket outside Los Angeles at 2028 Olympics
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The 2028 Los Angeles Olympics will feature baseball at Dodger Stadium in its return to the program with surfing and cricket held outside the city as part of an updated venue plan released Tuesday by the organizing committee.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tyron Smith set to retire with Cowboys after final season with Jets, AP source says
item.description: Tyron Smith is set to retire with the Dallas Cowboys after the perennial Pro Bowl left tackle spent his final season with the New York Jets, a person with knowledge of Smith's plans said Tuesday. The Cowboys set a Wednesday announcement from Smith without providing any details.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USA Fencing transgender controversy gets DOGE hearing after Stephanie Turner is punished for protest kneel
item.description: USA Fencing will be the subject of an upcoming DOGE hearing after an incident involving women's fencer Stephanie Turner kneeling in front of a trans opponent.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travis Hunter says he would 'never' play football again if an NFL team only let him play one side of the ball
item.description: Top NFL Draft prospect Travis Hunter said that would 'never play football again' if a team restricted him to only playing one side of the ball in the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau says Rory McIlroy 'didn't talk to me once' during final Masters round
item.description: Rory McIlroy apparently ignored his final pairing partner, Bryson DeChambeau, on a tense day at Augusta National, the LIV star revealed after Sunday's round.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Braves sideline reporter's effort to get woman's phone number sparks contentious online debate
item.description: An Atlanta Braves reporter's interaction with a woman during a game sparked an intense debate about journalism on Monday night and into Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PWHL picks Ottawa to host 3rd draft in June, with Wisconsin's Casey O'Brien among top prospects
item.description: The PWHL will hold its third annual entry draft in Ottawa on June 24, the league announced on Tuesday, with University of Wisconsin’s Casey O’Brien anticipated to be one of the top selections following her MVP senior season.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WNBA draftees turn attention to making rosters as training camp opens in a few weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Paige Bueckers will be heading to Dallas, the latest transcendent college star to go No. 1 in the WNBA draft. Her spot is assured with the Wings.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WNBA prospect Sedona Prince goes undrafted as abuse allegations surfaced in final college season
item.description: Former TCU Horned Frogs star Sedona Prince went undrafted on Monday night as the WNBA hopeful faced abuse allegations in her final college season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cubs can't hold lead, MLB-best Padres surge late to win opening game of 3-game series in San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Cubs' West Coast gauntlet against two of the best teams in baseball rolls on this week, with two games left.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl champ Don Hasselbeck dead at 70
item.description: Don Hasselbeck, who played nine years in the NFL and won a Super Bowl title with the Los Angeles Raiders, died at the age of 70, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fashion, heritage and talent: Players turn the WNBA draft into a cultural showcase
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA draft has increasingly become a platform for more than just athleticism — it is a celebration of identity, heritage, culture, and self-expression. Paige Bueckers may have been the No. 1 pick, but it was Kiki Iriafen who arguably stole the show when it came to fashion.
item.dc:creator: BRYANNA MAXWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Keller's four points leads Utah past Nashville, 7-3
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (ABC4 Sports) - With nothing to play for but pride, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season strong.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Nazar scores in shootout to give Blackhawks 4-3 win over Canadiens
item.description: Frank Nazar scored the only goal of the shootout, and the Chicago Blackhawks beat Montreal 4-3 on Monday night, spoiling Canadiens rookie Ivan Demidov's NHL debut.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Injury-prone Rockies DH Kris Bryant heads to IL for 9th time since joining the team in 2022
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Colorado Rockies designated hitter Kris Bryant has landed on the 10-day injured list with a bad back, the latest in a series of health issues that have resulted in nine IL stints since 2022. The team said the move, retroactive to Sunday, is due to lumbar degenerative disk disease.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paige Bueckers is No. 1 pick in WNBA draft, going to the Dallas Wings
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Paige Bueckers is ready for her next chapter after a whirlwind week that started with her helping UConn win its 12th national championship and ended with her becoming the WNBA's No. 1 draft pick by the Dallas Wings. “I’m just extremely excited to be there.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado will retire Shedeur Sanders’ No. 2 and Travis Hunter's No. 12 in ceremony at spring game
item.description: Shedeur Sanders and Travis Hunter left big footprints in only two seasons on the field at Colorado. For that, their numbers will be retired Saturday at the spring game as part of the afternoon's festivities. Sanders’ No. 2 and Hunter’s No.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago Sky select Ajsa Sivka, Hailey Van Lith in WNBA Draft
item.description: The Chicago Sky selected Slovenian stretch four Ajsa Sivka and TCU point guard Hailey Van Lith with their top two picks in the WNBA draft to address their need for perimeter shooting and to bolster their rebounding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Cam Ward leads group of former hidden gems turned NFL prospects
item.description: Back then, Ward worked behind center in his high school's run-heavy Wing-T offense. His roughly 12 throws per game didn't give him much chance to match the gaudy numbers of top recruits, and not many college coaches were making the one-hour drive from Houston to his hometown of roughly 4,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hailey Van Lith turns heads on WNBA Draft red carpet
item.description: Hailey Van Lith stunned on the WNBA Draft red carpet on Monday night before she heard her name called and she officially turned pro in New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz reflect on tough season, look to the future
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz finished the 2024-25 season not with just the worst record in the NBA, but the worst in franchise history. At 17-65, Jazz fans are extremely frustrated, but the Jazz brass insist brighter days are ahead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters playoff to complete the career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The closer Rory McIlroy came to fulfilling his lifetime dream — winning the Masters — the more it kept slipping away. Sunday at Augusta National felt like his last 11 years in the majors, blunders mixed in with sheer brilliance. A two-shot lead gone in two holes.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Cubs' Justin Steele to undergo elbow surgery, done for the season
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Cubs were dealt a heavy blow on the injury front Sunday evening. All-Star starting pitcher Justin Steele is done for the season, according to the Chicago Sun-Times.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Garrett Crochet dazzles in return to South Side, takes no-no into the 8th in Red Sox victory
item.description: Garrett Crochet returned to the South Side with a vengeance, throwing seven and one-third innings and striking out eleven, but the Red Sox won the game 3-1 to avoid a sweep.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rowdies lose homecoming matchup, but stadium comeback brings hope
item.description: For the first time in seven months, Rowdies fans are finally back home at Al Lang Stadium after the waterfront venue was impacted during back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Game 82: The end of the line for some in the NBA, decision day for others
item.description: Game 82 for every team in the NBA has arrived. For 10 clubs, it's the end of the season. For 13 others, it's one last game before the postseason. And for seven teams in the Western Conference, Game 82 might feel like Game 7.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thunder crush Jazz in home finale, 145-111
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - The Utah Jazz home finale was pretty representative of the entire season. The Jazz got blown out by the Oklahoma City Thunder.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fruita and Palisade show off defensive skill in 2-1 Wildcats win
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — We had an absolute showdown between two worthy Southwestern League foes at Suplizio Field Friday. Fruita pitchers Gavin Cardoza (1.62 ERA Friday) and Noah Brackett (0 ERA Friday) shut down Palisade's offense after a first-inning run.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Julian Phillips scores a career-high 23 points and Bulls rout Wizards 119-89
item.description: Julian Phillips scored a career-high 23 points on 7-of-9 shooting, Nikola Vucevic finished with 15 points, 12 rebounds and eight assists and the Chicago Bulls beat the Washington Wizards 119-89 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
